title: Tokyo 3-Day City Break
metaTitle: Tokyo 3-Day City Break | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 3-day trip to Tokyo, Japan: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including TeamLab Planets / Borderless and Tokyo Skytree. Prices from $1487 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 3 days discovering Tokyo, Japan — Humid subtropical climate with cold winters (highs 10°C, rarely snowy) and hot, humid summers (highs 31°C); the June rainy season (tsuyu) brings frequent showers. Late March to April (cherry blossoms) and October to November (autumn foliage) offer mild weather and stunning seasonal scenery. This itinerary blends TeamLab Planets / Borderless, Tokyo Skytree, Shibuya Crossing with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
2 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including TeamLab Planets / Borderless, Tokyo Skytree, Shibuya Crossing
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Tokyo
itinerary.description: Land at Haneda Airport (HND) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to TeamLab Planets / Borderless — Immersive digital art museum experience. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Tokyo: Tokyo Skytree & Tokyo Disneyland / DisneySea
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Tokyo Skytree — Tallest structure in Japan with observation decks. In the afternoon, continue to Tokyo Disneyland / DisneySea — Iconic theme parks, DisneySea unique to Japan. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Tokyo Tower or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to HND for your departure flight.

travelTips: Get a Suica or Pasmo IC card immediately for seamless train, bus and even konbini payments.
Reserve Ghibli Museum and popular sumo/TeamLab tickets weeks in advance — they sell out fast.
Avoid rush hour (7:30-9:30am) on the Yamanote and major commuter lines if traveling with luggage.
Carry cash — many restaurants, especially smaller ramen and izakaya spots, don't accept cards.
Convenience stores (7-Eleven, Lawson, FamilyMart) offer surprisingly excellent, cheap meals and are open 24/7.
With a short stay, book your must-see attractions in advance to avoid losing time to queues.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Insulated jacket and warm layers for cold weather
Comfortable shoes for extensive walking and train transfers
A cash supply — many smaller shops and restaurants remain cash-only
Layered clothing for seasonal temperature swings
A compact umbrella for the June rainy season
thingsToAvoid: Talking loudly or taking calls on trains — it's considered a serious breach of etiquette.
Tipping at restaurants — it is not customary and staff may try to return the money.
Walking and eating simultaneously in busy areas — generally frowned upon outside food festivals/markets.
Ignoring queue lines at train platforms — Japanese commuters queue precisely at marked positions.
Assuming English will be widely spoken outside major tourist hubs — a translation app helps significantly.
topRestaurants: Tsukiji Outer Market stalls — Seafood Street Food ($)
adventureActivities: Odaiba waterpark and go-karting (street-legal Mario Kart tours)
luxuryExperiences: Omakase dining at Sukiyabashi Jiro-caliber sushi counters
Stay at a luxury ryokan-style suite in the city
Private guided tour of the Tsukiji/Toyosu fish market at dawn
honeymoonExperiences: Private kaiseki dinner in a traditional setting
Couples visit to an onsen hot spring day spa
budgetExperiences: Free Shibuya Crossing and Senso-ji Temple visit
Convenience store (konbini) meals — genuinely excellent and cheap
Free observatory views from the Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building
localEtiquette: Bowing is the standard greeting; a slight nod is sufficient for visitors.
Remove shoes when entering homes, some restaurants and traditional accommodations.
Do not eat or talk loudly on trains, and keep phone calls off the train entirely.
Tipping is not customary and can be seen as confusing or even rude — it's simply not done.
Slurping noodles is acceptable and even appreciated; blowing your nose in public is considered impolite.
localTransportGuide: Tokyo's train and subway network (JR Yamanote Line plus Tokyo Metro/Toei lines) is extensive and punctual — a Suica or Pasmo IC card makes tapping through gates effortless. Taxis are reliable but pricier; walking is often just as fast for short hops within a single district.
bestTimeToVisit: Late March to April (cherry blossoms) and October to November (autumn foliage) offer mild weather and stunning seasonal scenery.
weather: Humid subtropical climate with cold winters (highs 10°C, rarely snowy) and hot, humid summers (highs 31°C); the June rainy season (tsuyu) brings frequent showers.
visaInformation: Visa-free entry for most nationalities including the US, UK, Canada, Australia and EU citizens for short tourist stays (typically up to 90 days). Verify current requirements against your specific passport before travel.
averageFlightTime: ~11.4h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Tokyo?
faqs.answer: Visa-free entry for most nationalities including the US, UK, Canada, Australia and EU citizens for short tourist stays (typically up to 90 days). Verify current requirements against your specific passport before travel.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Tokyo?
faqs.answer: Late March to April (cherry blossoms) and October to November (autumn foliage) offer mild weather and stunning seasonal scenery. Humid subtropical climate with cold winters (highs 10°C, rarely snowy) and hot, humid summers (highs 31°C); the June rainy season (tsuyu) brings frequent showers.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Tokyo safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Tokyo is rated very safe for tourists, with low rates of violent crime. Usual travel precautions (watch belongings in crowds, use licensed taxis) still apply.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Tokyo?
faqs.answer: The local currency is JPY. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Isabella Rossi
reviews.title: Exceeded our expectations
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Akihabara district and Senso-ji Temple (Nakamise-dori paid extras). Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Emma Novak
reviews.title: Worth every penny
reviews.body: Our trip to Tokyo was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Senso-ji Temple (Nakamise-dori paid extras) was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Mohammed Johnson
reviews.title: Best vacation we've had
reviews.body: Tokyo Tower alone was worth the trip, but combined with Yoyogi Park this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Lukas Andersen
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: Tokyo is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Meiji Jingu Museum. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Omar Silva
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Tokyo Disneyland / DisneySea and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Grace Smith
reviews.title: Beautiful destination, well organized
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Meiji Jingu Museum and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.
